text: Institute for Quality and Efficiency in Health Care | IQWiG. de The independent Institute for Quality and Efficiency in Health Care ( Institut für Qualität und Wirtschaftlichkeit im Gesundheitswesen, ) examines the benefits and harms of medical interventions for patients.
source: Institute for Quality and Efficiency in Health Care (IQWiG)

text: We provide information about the advantages and disadvantages of examination and treatment methods in the form of scientific reports and easily understandable health information. European benefit assessment of medicinal products countries have been cooperating more closely in the assessment of new medicinal products under the procedure, the European benefit assessment.
source: Institute for Quality and Efficiency in Health Care (IQWiG)

text: The aim is to provide Member States with a scientifically sound basis for decisions in their health systems. A significant step in the new EU HTA process: first assessment completed The new European benefit assessment has reached an important milestone: the first joint assessment, in which IQWiG was directly involved as the co-assessor,.
source: Institute for Quality and Efficiency in Health Care (IQWiG)

text: Registration now open: Launch of the new ‘IQWiG Forum Berlin’ event series On 25 March 2026, the first IQWiG Forum Berlin will kick off a new series of events aimed at intensifying the exchange between science and politics. Regis.
source: Institute for Quality and Efficiency in Health Care (IQWiG)

text: Registration now open: IQWiG's 3rd Information Retrieval Meeting in Cologne The 3rd Information Retrieval Meeting (IRM) will take place in Cologne on 24 April 2026 – registration is now open. The theme of the English-language eve. Easily understandable information for the general public InformedHealth. org is the English-language version of the German website Gesundheitsinformation. de.
source: Institute for Quality and Efficiency in Health Care (IQWiG)

text: By publishing this bilingual website, IQWiG fulfils part of its legal mandate to inform the general public about health issues. By offering a wide range of different topics, the website addresses both patients and (healthy) consumers. Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is quite common – even more common than asthma.
source: Institute for Quality and Efficiency in Health Care (IQWiG)

text: One of the typical symptoms is a persistent cough. In advanced COPD, it is difficult to breathe. At that point, even taking a walk or working in the garden can leave you out of breath. COPD develops gradually over the course of many years. People with schizophrenia have episodes of psychosis, where they lose touch with reality.
source: Institute for Quality and Efficiency in Health Care (IQWiG)

text: They may have delusions, hear voices or feel like they're being watched or controlled by others. Medication can reduce the symptoms, but psychological and social support are important too. Current commenting procedures and calls for tenders.
source: Institute for Quality and Efficiency in Health Care (IQWiG)
